feature_list.test_description: Verify direct support for 100 Kbps normal-mode I2C operation.
feature_list.test_constraints: Only 100 Kbps mode is specified for direct support; where implemented.
test_steps: Configure the I2C core for normal-mode operation at 100 Kbps.
Apply I2C traffic using SCL and SDA at the configured rate.
expected_results: The core operates at 100 Kbps normal-mode I2C.
Communication occurs without timing violation indicated by the interface behavior.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core can directly support 100 Kbps normal-mode I2C operation, where implemented.
feature_list.requirement_text:
The interface defines 3 transmission speeds:
- Normal: 100Kbps
Only 100Kbps and 400Kbps modes are supported directly.

feature_list.test_description: Verify direct support for 400 Kbps fast-mode I2C operation.
feature_list.test_constraints: Only 400 Kbps mode is specified for direct support; where correct hardware is included.
test_steps: Configure the I2C core for fast-mode operation at 400 Kbps.
Apply I2C traffic using SCL and SDA at the configured rate.
expected_results: The core operates at 400 Kbps fast-mode I2C.
Communication occurs without timing violation indicated by the interface behavior.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core can support 400 Kbps fast-mode I2C operation, where correct hardware is included.
feature_list.requirement_text:
The interface defines 3 transmission speeds:
- Fast: 400Kbps
Only 100Kbps and 400Kbps modes are supported directly.

feature_list.test_description: Verify the core exposes a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface.
feature_list.test_constraints: Limited to the interface signals and compliance statement provided.
test_steps: Connect the core to a WISHBONE Classic bus environment using the listed WISHBONE signals.
Perform a standard bus access using wb_clk_i, wb_cyc_i, wb_stb_i, wb_we_i, wb_adr_i, and wb_dat_i as applicable.
expected_results: The interface responds through wb_dat_o and wb_ack_o as a WISHBONE Classic peripheral.
The interface is consistent with WISHBONE RevB.3 operation.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core shall implement a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface.
feature_list.requirement_text: The core features a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface.

feature_list.test_description: Verify each WISHBONE access takes two clock cycles.
feature_list.test_constraints: Timing is measured on wb_clk_i with valid WISHBONE access conditions.
test_steps: Initiate a valid WISHBONE access using wb_cyc_i and wb_stb_i.
Count wb_clk_i cycles until wb_ack_o indicates completion.
expected_results: wb_ack_o asserts on the second clock cycle of the access.
Each access completes in exactly two clock cycles.
feature_list.requirement_description: Each WISHBONE access must take two clock cycles.

feature_list.test_description: Verify asynchronous reset operates with active-high ARST_LVL configuration.
test_steps: Assert arst_i high and observe reset behavior.
expected_results: The core enters reset when arst_i is high.
The core leaves reset when arst_i is deasserted.
feature_list.requirement_description: The asynchronous reset level is configurable as active high or active low.
feature_list.requirement_text: The asynchronous reset level can be set to either active high (1’b1) or active low (1’b0)

feature_list.test_description: Verify asynchronous reset operates with active-low ARST_LVL configuration.
test_steps: Assert arst_i low and observe reset behavior.
expected_results: The core enters reset when arst_i is low.
The core leaves reset when arst_i is deasserted.
feature_list.requirement_description: The asynchronous reset level is configurable as active high or active low.
feature_list.requirement_text: The asynchronous reset level can be set to either active high (1’b1) or active low (1’b0)

feature_list.test_description: Verify SCL pad input, output, and output-enable signals are exposed for external open-drain bus connection.
feature_list.test_constraints: Limited to SCL-related pad signals listed in the specification.
test_steps: Check that scl_pad_i, scl_pad_o, and scl_pad_oe are present at the core interface.
Drive and observe SCL-related pad signals in an external connection setup.
expected_results: The SCL pad input, output, and output-enable signals are available.
The SCL connection can be represented through the listed pad signals.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core must expose SCL and SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals for external open-drain bus connection.
feature_list.requirement_text:
scl_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Clock line input
scl_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output
scl_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output enable
sda_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Data line input
sda_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output
sda_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output enable

feature_list.test_description: Verify SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals are exposed for external open-drain bus connection.
feature_list.test_constraints: Limited to SDA-related pad signals listed in the specification.
test_steps: Check that sda_pad_i, sda_pad_o, and sda_pad_oe are present at the core interface.
Drive and observe SDA-related pad signals in an external connection setup.
expected_results: The SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals are available.
The SDA connection can be represented through the listed pad signals.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core must expose SCL and SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals for external open-drain bus connection.
feature_list.requirement_text:
scl_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Clock line input
scl_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output
scl_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output enable
sda_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Data line input
sda_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output
sda_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output enable
